what_it_does: This file is a grounded synthesis document that maps the 'Right-Hand-Man brain' concept — an inbuilt Claude Code instance bound to an address and accessed through the V UI — onto what is actually built, what is only designed, and what file/line evidence supports each claim. It also defines the 'keystone' unbuilt generate-write seam and explains how composition should plug into that single circuit rather than build a parallel one.
observations: Uses explicit evidence labels: Observed, Inferred, Verified.
Cautions that 'loadable brain' phrasing is a fabric working term, not a Tim verbatim quote.
Provides file:line anchors such as RightHand.tsx:62, bridge.py:1696, territory.py:152.
Includes a built-vs-designed-only table with honest status markers.
Identifies the 'keystone' as the unbuilt generate/change write-back leg.
Describes per-address session continuity in fork-brain-core.js.
Notes the PANEL_BRIEFING operator-law fix preventing raw addresses in replies.
Gives concrete composition integration steps in §4.
Lists open questions without answering them.
standouts: The 'keystone' concept: one unbuilt rung (generate-write) that unlocks the whole change/generate capability.
Composition must build INTO the existing brain circuit, not create a parallel streamer.
Strong provenance caution distinguishing Tim's verbatim from Claude-injected compaction summaries.
Per-address conversation continuity makes each aimed element its own thread.
conventions: Transcript line-number citations for Tim quotes.
Honest status markers: BUILT, VERIFIED-BY-USE, DESIGNED-ONLY, SEAM stubs.
Structured tables and ASCII wiring diagrams.
concerns: Many claims depend on transcript line numbers and file contents not reproduced here, so they cannot be verified from this file alone.
The distinction between Observed and Inferred is noted but the reader must still trust the author's mining.
Provisional disclaimer says 'Tim is the judge of right,' which signals the document itself is not authoritative.
notes: A self-aware build-prep synthesis that tries to stop parallel invention by showing exactly which RHM brain legs are live and which are still design-only, especially the generate-write keystone.
questions: What exactly does the keystone generate-write payload and endpoint look like in code?
Is vi-vision:// the final agreed address scheme for composition's slot/catalog registry?
How will the mind-swap / N+1 mode mechanism be implemented beyond 'same mechanism, interchangeable composition'?
What are the concrete designs for follow-me and piggy-back modes?
How will the real V organism be swapped into the RightHand container in practice?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a grounded synthesis of the RHM brain from transcripts, build-prep, and memory; the file does exactly that, including honest gaps and integration guidance.
apparent_themes: Grounded honesty about built vs unbuilt state.
Single-circuit retargeting by address rather than parallel systems.
Context resolution never kills the turn.
Build into existing seams, not around them.
intention_signals: The file is trying to align the team on what the RHM brain actually is today, prevent duplicated brain/streamer implementations, and give composition a clear, non-parallel integration path through the one unbuilt generate-write seam.
novelty: It frames Claude Code as an address-bound, loadable brain inside the V overlay and uses the 'keystone' metaphor to isolate a single missing rung that would turn a record-only write-back into a full generate/change circuit.
contribution: It contributes a transparent, evidence-typed inventory of the RHM brain's current state plus a concrete integration recipe for composition, which a fused system could keep as a living map of seams and build targets.
summary_for_embedding: A grounded synthesis document mapping the Right-Hand-Man brain concept to built code, designed-only gaps, file/line evidence, and the single unbuilt generate-write seam that composition should plug into.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an honest, integration-oriented status map of the RHM brain so teams do not build parallel circuits and know exactly which seam to complete.
